pr.title: lb: name THP header fields and float constants in lbmthp
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Readability/naming follow-up for `lbmthp`: `THPDecComp` THP header unknowns were renamed to semantic fields, repeated float literals were represented as named `.sdata2` constants, and `fn_8001EBF0` gained a small fuzzy-match improvement from 98.77% to 99.23% by folding `ALIGN_32` into a single assignment. No new 100% function matches were claimed. Reviewer approved but cautioned that data-section matching is generally not worth prioritizing before the whole translation unit's code is matched.

key_files.role: Renamed `THPDecComp` header fields at offsets 0x08-0x28 from `unk_*` placeholders to THP header names.
Propagated the new field names through THP load/init, frame-buffer setup, diagnostics, alarm setup, texture LOD setup, and the `fn_8001EBF0` ALIGN_32 matching tweak.
Replaced unused placeholder static floats with named const `.sdata2` labels `lbl_804D7CC8`, `lbl_804D7CD0`, and `lbl_804D7CD8`.

what_changed: `THPDecComp` fields `unk_08` through `unk_28` were renamed to `version`, `buf_size`, `x_size`, `y_size`, `frame_rate`, `num_frames`, `first_frame`, `frame_offsets`, and `first_frame_size` while preserving offsets and padding.
`fn_8001EB14` now copies THP header values using the semantic names: `num_frames`, `x_size`, `y_size`, and `buf_size`, and checks `frame_offsets` and `version` instead of unnamed fields.
`fn_8001ECF4` now uses `first_frame_size` and `first_frame` for frame-buffer/file-offset setup and prints the named header fields in its diagnostic `OSReport` block.

`lbmthp.static.h` introduced `static const float lbl_804D7CC8 = 0.016666668f`, `static const f64 lbl_804D7CD0 = 4503599627370496e0`, and `static const float lbl_804D7CD8 = 0.0f`, replacing placeholder symbols `lb_804D7CC0` and `lbl_804D7CE0`.
`lbMthp_8001F410` now uses `lbl_804D7CC8` for the periodic alarm timing calculation instead of spelling `0.016666668f` inline.
`lbMthp_8001F67C` now uses `lbl_804D7CD8` for the three `GXInitTexObjLOD` zero-float arguments instead of inline `0.0f` literals.

`fn_8001EBF0` changed `aligned_100 = data->unk_100; aligned_100 = ALIGN_32(aligned_100);` into `aligned_100 = ALIGN_32(data->unk_100);`, which the PR body and decomp.dev bot report as improving the fuzzy match from 98.77% to 99.23%.

Verification reported `python configure.py && ninja` green with `build/GALE01/main.dol: OK`, clean builds for consumers including `mn` and `gm`, and preserved match status for previously matched `lbmthp.c` functions.
smart_moves: Renamed only fields with clear THP-header evidence and left unrelated unresolved members such as `unk_40`, `unk_100`, and stream-player fields as unknowns rather than guessing.
Kept struct offsets visible in `lbmthp.h`, making the semantic rename safe for a binary-matching decompilation workflow.
Propagated names through runtime copies and error-reporting paths, so future readers can connect header offsets to actual behavior such as dimensions, frame counts, and first-frame file offsets.
Used one direct `ALIGN_32(data->unk_100)` assignment in `fn_8001EBF0`, a small source-shape change that improved the fuzzy score without changing the visible logic.
Recorded that no new 100% function matches were produced, preventing the PR from being misclassified as a major matching milestone.
decomp_lessons: For struct cleanup PRs, preserve exact offset comments and padding while replacing `unk_*` names with format/container terminology when there is clear evidence.
When header field names are introduced, update all diagnostic and copy sites in the same PR; this makes debug `OSReport` blocks useful evidence for later decomp work.
Tiny expression-shape changes can affect codegen and fuzzy matching; folding a transform into the initial assignment may be worth trying for unmatched functions.
Named float/data labels can improve readability, but reviewer feedback says data-section matching should not be a priority before the full translation unit's code is matched.
Keep build verification explicit when public headers change, especially when other modules consume the header.
naming_conventions: `THPDecComp` header fields use plain THP/container names: `version`, `buf_size`, `x_size`, `y_size`, `frame_rate`, `num_frames`, `first_frame`, `frame_offsets`, `first_frame_size`.
Header dimensions are named `x_size` and `y_size`, while runtime copies remain `width` and `height`.
Frame-related names distinguish count, offset table, first-frame offset, and first-frame compressed size: `num_frames`, `frame_offsets`, `first_frame`, `first_frame_size`.
Unresolved non-header fields remain in `unk_*` form, which avoids over-naming uncertain data.
Static data symbols kept address-label names such as `lbl_804D7CC8`, `lbl_804D7CD0`, and `lbl_804D7CD8` rather than receiving speculative semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `fn_8001EBF0`: using `aligned_100 = ALIGN_32(data->unk_100);` instead of assigning then aligning the temporary improved the unmatched-item fuzzy score from 98.77% to 99.23%.
The diff also reorders local declarations around `aligned_100`, `width`, and `height`; evidence only confirms this accompanied the fuzzy-score improvement, not that it alone caused it.
Replacing inline float literals with `.sdata2` labels made code reference `lbl_804D7CC8` for `0.016666668f` and `lbl_804D7CD8` for `0.0f` in alarm/texture setup.
No new 100% function match was achieved, and all previously matched functions were reported preserved.

review_feedback.feedback: It is fine to merge, but generally a waste of time to try to get the data section to match before the code for the entire translation unit is matched.
review_feedback.lesson: Do not over-prioritize `.sdata2` or data-section cleanup while the containing TU still has unmatched code.

review_feedback.feedback: Author acknowledged the concern and said a new workflow opened/committed the PR before they had a chance to catch it.
review_feedback.lesson: Workflow automation should not accidentally publish low-priority data-section-only cleanup before review intent is clear.
The PR had measurable but minor matching impact.
